user_query:
We’re planning to publish v1.4.3 of our CLI today, but I want you to run a full pre-release quality gate first and decide if we can proceed. Read all inputs from input/ and write all artifacts under output/.

Inputs to read:
- input/current-time.txt (ISO timestamp for "now", use this to compute the 24h cooldown)
- input/last-release.json (last released version and ISO timestamp)
- input/release-history.json (recent releases with timestamps for pattern checks)
- input/feedback.json (downloads/issues/user notes, summarize real user feedback on the previous version)
- input/README.md (docs check)
- input/CHANGELOG.md (ensure it includes a v1.4.3 section)
- input/SOUL.md (project principles; quote any relevant principle if there’s a contradiction)
- input/kill-criteria.md (confirm kill criteria are defined)
- input/release-plan.json (target version and the ONE thing this release does better than the last)

What to do:
1) Run a 6-gate pre-release checklist (cooldown, user feedback, documentation, quality/substance, kill criteria, self-contradiction). For each gate, decide PASS, WARNING, or BLOCKED and give a short, specific reason.
2) Cooldown calculation must use input/current-time.txt and input/last-release.json, expressed as hours since last release with one decimal precision.
3) Documentation must verify both README.md and that CHANGELOG.md has a v1.4.3 section.
4) Quality gate must verify release-plan.json has a clear ONE thing (not vague like "minor fixes").
5) Self-contradiction must consider SOUL.md and recent release frequency from input/release-history.json; if there’s a conflict, quote the principle that’s violated.
6) Decide overall status: APPROVED, WARNING, or BLOCKED (if any gate is BLOCKED, overall must be BLOCKED). Do NOT actually publish; just decide.

Required outputs:
- output/decision.txt — First word must be exactly one of APPROVED, WARNING, or BLOCKED, followed by a colon and a succinct one-line reason.
- output/gate-report.json — JSON with keys: version, status, hours_since_last_release (number with one decimal), and a gates object with keys cooldown, user_feedback, documentation, quality, kill_criteria, self_contradiction. Each gate entry must have fields: result (PASS|WARNING|BLOCKED) and reason (non-empty string). Include a user_feedback_summary string referencing concrete items from input/feedback.json.
- output/release-log.md — Append or create a structured entry for v1.4.3 including: Status line (with emoji if you use them), a Gates line that shows all six gates in order (1..6) with their results, a Reason section, a short user feedback summary, and "Time since last release: X.Y hours" using the same one-decimal value.

Be strict but fair. If blocked, recommend what to fix next. Use only the input files listed and make no assumptions beyond them.
